assembly_instructions: Compact open-source underwater ROV. A flat acrylic chassis base plate carries a central cylindrical acrylic electronics tube with a capped forward end, flanked by two smaller cylindrical battery tubes. Three brushless thrusters — two pointing aft on either side and one vertical through the centerline — give six-DOF control. A forward CMOS camera and an adjacent LED ring sit ahead of the electronics tube. A neutrally-buoyant tether rises up and aft to the surface controller, a ballast bar hangs below the base plate for trim, and a control board plus motor-controller module ride on top of the chassis.

info.summary: Tether-operated DIY underwater ROV from the OpenROV community. Acrylic electronics housing, three brushless thrusters, forward camera + LEDs, ~100 m depth rating.
info.description: OpenROV 2.8 is the last of the 'classic' OpenROV tethered ROVs — a citizen-science and maker-oriented underwater vehicle released as an open-hardware kit. The flat laser-cut acrylic chassis carries a central watertight electronics tube (housing a BeagleBone Black + custom cape running the open OpenROV Cockpit software), two side-mounted battery tubes, and three brushless thrusters (two horizontal at the rear for surge/yaw, one vertical through the centerline for heave). A forward CMOS camera on a small tilt servo pairs with a high-output LED array for visibility in turbid water. The tether is a neutrally buoyant twisted-pair that carries Ethernet-over-powerline back to a topside router and laptop running the control UI. Nominal depth rating is around 100 m; runtime on internal C-cells is roughly 2–3 hours. The hardware and software are released under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
info.operator: OpenROV community / Sofar Ocean builders and educators
info.status: Classic 2.x production ended; superseded by Trident. Open-hardware designs remain public.

facts.label: Lighting
facts.value: High-output LED array beside the camera

facts.label: Runtime
facts.value: ≈2–3 h on C-cells per battery tube

parts.name: Chassis Base Plate
parts.role: Laser-cut acrylic base plate that ties all modules together.

parts.name: Electronics Tube
parts.role: Central watertight acrylic housing for the BeagleBone controller, cape, and tilt servo.

parts.name: Acrylic End Cap (Front)
parts.role: Machined endcap that seals the forward end of the electronics tube with O-rings.

parts.name: Battery Tube (Port)
parts.role: Watertight acrylic tube carrying C-cells for port-side power channel.

parts.name: Battery Tube (Starboard)
parts.role: Watertight acrylic tube carrying C-cells for starboard-side power channel.

parts.name: Thruster (Rear Port, horizontal)
parts.role: Rear horizontal brushless thruster, port side — generates surge/yaw.

parts.name: Thruster (Rear Starboard, horizontal)
parts.role: Rear horizontal brushless thruster, starboard side — generates surge/yaw.

parts.name: Thruster (Center, vertical)
parts.role: Center vertical brushless thruster — generates heave (up/down).

parts.name: Propeller (representative)
parts.role: Small ducted propeller at the aft end of each thruster — one modeled here as representative.

parts.name: Forward CMOS Camera
parts.role: Forward-facing CMOS camera on a small tilt servo.

parts.name: LED Light Array
parts.role: High-output LED array beside the camera — lights the scene in turbid water.

parts.name: Tether Cable
parts.role: Neutrally buoyant twisted-pair tether. Carries Ethernet-over-powerline up to a topside router and laptop.

parts.name: Control Board (BBB + Cape)
parts.role: BeagleBone Black + custom OpenROV cape running the Cockpit control stack. Shown on top of the electronics tube for inspectability.

parts.name: Motor Controller
parts.role: Brushless ESC module that drives all three thrusters.

parts.name: Ballast / Trim Weight
parts.role: Small steel bar hung under the base plate to set neutral buoyancy and trim.
